reason: Both answers correctly identify the shift away from model quality toward workflow depth, data loops, distribution, and switching costs, and both note the 6-month lead should be reinvested rather than defended. Answer A adds a "multi-model arbitrage" point and closes with named company examples (Jasper vs. Cursor/Harvey) that are speculative and not clearly substantiated, adding some risk without much payoff. Answer B covers the same core moats and additionally introduces "domain-specific evaluation & reliability" (guardrails/evals for niche correctness) — a genuinely distinct and highly relevant defensibility lever for applied AI products — plus concrete distribution details (SOC 2/HIPAA) and a sharper closing framing ("assume the 6-month lead is already gone"). B is slightly more precise and avoids the unverifiable example-dropping in A, while covering an equal or greater share of the key points.
